more: n. m. jūtī , n. f. 1. A shoe; slipper; a pair of shoes. 1. Tumhārī jūtī merā sir . Wom. Your shoes on my head. (Shoe-beat me if I am in fault). 2. Usī kī jūtī, usī kā sir . Prov. His own head (beaten) with his own shoes. (The engineer hoist with his own petard). 3. Mard kā kyā hai, ek jūtī paknī, ek jūtī utārī . Wom. Prov. It's nothiṅg for a man, he puts on one shoe and throws off another. (He gets a new wife and turns away the old). 4. Yār ātā hai merā naṅge pāoṅ; Jī meṅ ātā hai uṭh-ke de jūtā . * A jest. 5. Jiske sir par jūtā rakh diyā, vohī bādshāh ho gayā . Prov. On whom I choose my shoes to lay shall vested be with regal sway. 6. Panchoṅ kā jūtā, aur merā sir . Prov. The Panch's shoes upon my head. (I am all ready to do their bidding). 7. Āp hī kī jūtiyoṅ kā sadqā hai . Prov. ☞ The story goes that a wag, taking advantage of the religious obligation ( sunnat ) which requires Mahomada…

ety: Prk. किअउं; S. कृतं, perf. part. neut. of कृ 'to do,' &c.
def: postpn. m. (f. kī; obl. & pl. ke), Of; belonging to; concerning; made of, consisting of, &c. (kā is the postpn. of the gen. case in Urdū and Hindī, and, as such, is also frequently used as an adj. termination, e.g. sone-kī ghaṛī, 'a gold watch'; kāṭh-kā ghoṛā, 'a wooden horse'; in the case of the pronouns of the first and second persons, kā takes the form rā; and in some dialects of Hindī rā is the gen. postpn. of nouns as well as pronouns; while in others lā is found in place of kā and rā; cf. also kar, karā, ker, and kerā, which are rustic forms of kā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit किअउं (kiaüṃ), from Sanskrit कृतं (kṛtaṃ) (perfect participle neuter of कृ (kṛ, “to do”)). Dasa also suggests क (ka, diminutive suffix). Compare Hindi का (kā) and Punjabi دا (dā).

def: a Taddhita affix much used in forming adjectives; it is also added to nouns to express diminutiveness, depreciation, contempt, &c. (taking, in Persian words, the more common form of ak), and also similarity, or resemblance.

r: kā, ka, ku, ka-pūt, ku-putr, kā-purush
def: = H ك क ka, = S ك कु ku, A prefix implying uselessness, badness, deterioration, depreciation, defectiveness, &c. (e.g. ka-pūt, or ku-putr, 'a bad son'; kā-purush, 'a vile man; a coward').

1. ( क the termination of attributive words) postpos. Of, belonging to. 2. ( कः ) in Braj, pron. inflect. sing. of کون or کو Whom? what? which?

def: s.f. A boys' game resembling our 'Prisoners' base' (kabaḍḍī kabaḍḍī is the cry of the boy who runs out to touch or capture an adversary).
more: s. f. A game among boys, who divide themselves into two parties; one of which takes its station on one side of a line or ridge, called pālā, made on the ground, and the other on the other. One boy, shouting kabaḍḍī kabaḍḍī, passes this line, and endeavours to touch one of those on the opposite side. If he is able to do this, and to return to his own party, the boy that was so touched is supposed to be slain; that is, he retires from the game. But if the boy who made the assault be seized and unable to return, he dies, or retires in the same manner. The assault is thus made from the two sides alternately, and that party is victorious of which some remain after all their opponents are slain. (Prison bars?).

def: n. f. S. क + बृध. A kind of 'Prisoners' base.' The dividing line is called pālā . One of the party from one side calling out ' kabaḍḍī kabaḍḍī ' encroaches upon the other side and tries to touch one of his opponents and return to his own side without taking breath. If he succeeds his antagonist is 'out,' if not he is out of the game. The opposite side meanwhile try to make a prisoner of him. 1. Kabaḍḍī tīn tāre, shitāb yār māre . Boys. 2. Jījī! oh to kabaḍḍī ghāltā hāṅḍe saë, tauṅh kahe thī ausakh māṅ paṛā saë . Rus. Why, sister! he is skipping about all over the place, you said he was laid up. kabaḍḍī khelte phirnā , v. n. To wander or saunter about. kabaḍḍī khelnā , v. a. To play kabaḍḍī .

Payable (a bill, etc.); due; owing; liable for. qābil-i-adā , n. Solvent. qābil izāfa lagān . Liable to enhancement of rent. qābil-i-itlāq ; H. lagtā , Applicable. qābil-i-etibār ; H. parlīt-jog , Credible; worthy of credit; reliable. qāibl-i-etirāz ; H. tarak-jog ; Objectionable; open to objection. qābil-i-iltifāt ; H. dhiyān-jog , Worthy of reception or attention. qābil-i-intiqāl ; H. palaṭne-jog , Transferrable; capable of being transferred. qābil-i-inām , Deserving a prize. qābil-i-bāz-purs ; H. uttar-jog , Answerable; accountable; responsible. qābil-i-bai wa sharٴa ; H. bisāhne bechne-jog , Negotiable. qābil-i-pizīrāī ; H. mānne-jog , Receivable; admissible; that may be accepted. qābil-i-pizīrāī ke nahīṅ , Inadmissible. qābil-i-pasaṅd . Eligible; choice; good. qābil-i-tabdīl , Variable; liable to change or modification. qābil-i-tardīd , Liable to be contested; that may be refuted. qābil-i-taslīm . Presumable; probable; that which may be taken for granted; worthy of acceptation. qābil-i-tasfīyah ; H. chuktāne-jog , Open to adjustment. qābil-i-tٴadād ; H. ginne jog . Computable. qābil-i-tārīf, tārīf ke qābil ; H. astuti-jog , Creditable; estimable; deserving praise. qābil-i-taqsīm, yāinqisām ; H. baṭne-jog , Divisible. qābil-i-tavajjoh . Worthy of attention or consideration. qābila-i-taurīs ; H. bapautī-jog , Descendible; heritable. qābil-i-ṭaiks . Leviable; liable to tax; assessable. qābil-i-javāb . Answerable; accountable. qābīl-i-javāz . G. G. Admissible; allowable. qābil dāir hone ke, yā rujū karne ke . May be instituted; actionable. qābil-i-zirāٴat, yā taraddud ; H. bone-jog , Culturable but not cultivated; arable; fit for cultivation. qābil-i-zirāٴat k. , To fertilize; to clear (waste land); to make culturable. qābil-i-sazā , Culpable; liable to be punished ( ḍaṅḍ-jog ). qābil-i-samāٴat yā sunvāī ; H. sunne-jog . Capable of being heard; that may be admitted or entertained. qābil-i-zabtī . Liable to forfeiture or confiscation. qābil-i-talab ; H. māṅgne-jog . Leviable; what may be demanded. qābil-i-gaur samajhnā , v. a. To attach importance to. qābil gaur ke ; H. bichār-jog , Considerable; worthy of consideration; important. qābil-i-farokht ; H. bechne-jog . Saleable. qābil-i-fanā ; H. miṭne-jog , Perishable. qābil qurqī ke murtakib h ., To commit an act of forfeiture. qābil-i-mākhūzī . Chargeable; indictable; penal. qābil-i-mākhūzī-i-faujdārī . Liable to criminal proceedings. qābil-i-mahsūl ; H. lagān-jog . Subject to taxation; leviable. qābil-i-muāfī , Pardonable (H. chhimā-jog ). qābil-i-māzūrī . Veniable; justifiable; pardonable. qābil-i-manzūrī . Sanctionable; fit to be chosen. qābil manzūrī-i-zamānat . Bailable. qābil nālish-i-faujdārī . Liable to criminal proceedings. qābil-i-nikāh ; H. bar-jog, byāhan-jog . Marraiageable. qābil-i-vusūl ; H. pāne-jog , Receivable; recoverable. qābil-i-vaqūٴa . Incidental; apt to happen; likely to occur. qābil h. , v. n. To be competent, proficient, skilled.
prop. n. Cain (son of Adam)
s.m. Family, race, tribe; kindred; progeny; — species, kind, class; category
more: n. m. Class; genus (ذات 4). qabīl-i-anās , Ped. G. G. The female sex. az qabīl . Of the nature of. min qabīl unās . Of the female sex.
(from قبل) s. m. 1. Species, kind, sort. 2. Family, tribe, kindred, progeny.
subs: or min qabīl, Of the class, or kind, &c.; of the nature (of).

ety: for A. قابلة qābilat, fem. of qābil
def: s.f. A clever or skilful woman, &c.; — a midwife; — a male screw; a bolt.
more: n. m. 1. ( kāblā ), The male screw. 2. A bolt. 3. A midwife.

ety: for A. قبيلة qabīlat, fem. of qabīl
def: s.m. Tribe, clan; family; wife
more: n. m. 1. A tribe; clan. 2. Wife; family.
s. m. 1. A family, a tribe. 2. A wife.

def: s. (dakh.) See قابليت ḳābil-īyat.

ety: for A. قابلية; qābil+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Aptitude to receive, capacity, capability, fitness, suitableness; skill, ability, power; sufficiency; possibility.
more: n. f. 1. Capability; power (استعداد 1). 2. Possibility; sufficiency. 3. Fitness; suitableness. qābīliyat paidā k. , v. a. To acquire capacity or ability. qāblīyat-i-inqisām ; H. baṭaunī , Divisibility.
s. f. Possibility, skill, sufficiency, capacity, capability, ability, accomplishment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَابِلِیَّت (qābiliyyat), from Arabic قَابِلِيَّة (qābiliyya). Equivalent of قابل (qābil, “able, capable”) + یت (iyyat, “ism, ility”).

def: s.m. Marriage-portion, settlement (on a wife), jointure, dower; — kābīn-nāma, s.m. A marriage settlement, a deed of dower.
more: n. m. A marriage portion; settlement on a wife; a jointure. kābīn-nāma, mahr-nāma , n. f. A deed of dower; a marriage settlement.
s. m. A marriage portion or settlement, jointure, dower.

def: adv. (colloq. or dialec.), corr. of kabhīṅ, q.v. adv. Sometime or other, sometimes; at any time, ever; ever so long ago
subs: (-kī, -ke), adj. & adv. Of sometime; sometime ago, long since
adv. At one time — at another time; now — now
adv. Sometimes, now and then, at times, occasionally
adv. Sometime or other, sooner or later, first or last; rarely, seldom

ety: act. part. of کبر 'to be large or great,' &c.
def: adj. Great, grand, illustrious (cf. kabīr).
more: (part. act. of کبر Heb. ) adj. Great, grand, illustrious.

def: adj. & s.m. Great, large, immense; full-grown; senior; — a nobleman, a grandee; — name of a great Hindū reformer, saint, and author who flourished about the close of the fifteenth century; — a kind of indecent song (sung by the lower classes)
more: adj. کبر Opp of صغير q. v. 1. Great. 2. Senior. kabīr , n. m. 1. ( kabīrā ) The greatest Hindu reformer, eclectic philosopher, and popular poet and epigrammatist, who lashed alike the follies of Mahomedans and Hindus, and was the idol of both. 2. E. A kind of indecent Hindī song, sung by the lower classes.
(Heb. from کبر) adj. 1. Large, immense, great, full-grown, senior. 2. n. prop. Name of a faḳīr. کبير پنتهي kabīr-panthī, s. m. A follower of Kabīr. کبير کا الٿا kabīr kā ulṭā, Transposition.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کبیر (kabīr), from Arabic كَبِير (kabīr).

def: s.m. = kabīr, q.v. adj. Great, grand, illustrious (cf. kabīr).

ety: for A. کبيرة kabīrat, fem. of kabīr
def: s.m. A great or heinous sin.
s.m. A follower of Kabīr ( = kabīr-panthī).
adj. Quick, expeditious (esp. applied to a stallion camel).
s.f. An earth with which earthenware is varnished.
more: s. f. An earth with which earthenware is varnished.

ety: for A. کبيسة kabīsat, fr. کبس 'to fill up'
def: adj. Intercalary
more: (from کبس To fill up a well, &c.) s. m. Intercalary. سال کبيسه sāl-i-kabīsa, Leap-year.

def: s.m. See s.v. kabi. s.m. A wise man; a poet
subs: or kabīśwar (˚vi+īś˚), s.m. The chief of the wise men; prince of poets, poet-laureate; an eminent poet (syn. malikuʼsh-shuʻarā).

def: s.m. See kavīśvar, and kabīsar.
more: s. m. An eminent poet, prince of poets.

def: adj. & s.m. Niggardly, miserly; — a niggard, a miser.
more: adj. Niggardly. kabīsar , n. m. A miser.

def: s.f. An earth with which earthenware is varnished.
s.m. See s.v. kabi. s.m. A wise man; a poet
subs: or kabīśwar (˚vi+īś˚), s.m. The chief of the wise men; prince of poets, poet-laureate; an eminent poet (syn. malikuʼsh-shuʻarā).

def: s.m. See kavīśvar, and kabīsar.
more: Mah. n. m. A prince of poets; poet laureate. Sārā! chār kabat sīkh-kar kabīsar ho gayā! Rus. This pimp to his sister has just learnt four couplets, and lo! a prince of poets he is grown!

def: s.m. A sort of verse in Hindī poetry; a poem, poetry, song.
more: n. m. कबिता A kind of Hindī verse of four lines. Kabit sohe bhāṭ ne, aur khetī sohe Jāṭ ne . Rus. Prov. The bard shines in his minstrelsy, the Jāṭ shines in his husbandry.
s. m. A sort of verse used by the Hindūs, poetry.

def: s.m. A poet.
more: n. f. Poetry; a poem. kabtāī k. , v. a. To make Sanskrit or Hin. verses.

def: s.f. Poetry; a poem
subs: To compose verses (in Sanskrit or Hindī).

def: s.f. Poetry; a poem
subs: To compose verses (in Sanskrit or Hindī).

ety: act. part. of قبض 'to take, seize, grasp,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Taking, seizing, grasping, holding, possessing; — astringent; constipating; — taker, seizer, &c.; holder, possessor, occupier, occupant, party in possession; a sequestrator; — an astringent, a constipator
more: n. قبض taking. 1. Possessor; holder. 2. Occupant; occupier; the party in possession. 3. A sequestrator. qābiz , adj. 1. Possessing. 2. Costive. qābiz-i-aslī, yā haqīqī . The actual possessor. qābiz o dakhīl , In possession and enjoyment (of an esṭate). qābiz aur mālik . 1. Owner and occupant. 2. Proprietory possessor. qābiz barāë nām . The ostensible possessor; nominal holder. qābiz-i-hāl . The present possessor; the party in actual possession. qābiz-i-hīn-hayālī . A life-tenant. qābiz-i-shikmī . An under-tenant; a sub-lessee. qābiz ho baiṭhnā , v. n. To take possession; settle on another' lands.
(part. act. from قبض) 1. adj. Seizing, holding, grasping, taking. 2. Astringent. 3. s. m. A receiver, possessor. قابض الارواح ḳābiẓu-l-arwāḥ, The angel of death.
subs: s.m. 'The seizer of souls,' the angel of death
or qābiẓ ho-baithnā (-par), To seize (on), get hold (of), take possession (of); to enter on occupation (of), or settle (upon, another's land).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قَابِض (qābiḍ).

def: s.m. A species of partridge, or quail
more: n. m. A bird of the partridge or quail kind; Tetrao coturnix ; the snow pheasant.
s. m. Name of a bird of the partridge kind. کبک دري kabk-i-darī, s. m. A beautiful partridge. کبک رفتار kabk-raftār, adj. (Graceful as) the kabk in motion.
subs: The mountain partridge, Tetrao coturnix (i.q. kabk); the Greek partridge, or ćakor
adj. Graceful as the partridge in gait or motion.

def: s.m. The anterior or fore-part, the front; — adj. Anterior, prior, first; preliminary, preparatory; — prep. & adv. (of time), Previous (to, -ke), prior (to), before
more: adj. 1. Previous; anterior (سابق). 2. Preliminary; preparatory. qabl-i-ikhtitām-i-nīlām . Prior to the lot being knocked down. qabl az āṅ-ki . Before that; ante quam ; previously; prior to; beforehand. qabl az īṅ, is se pahle . Before this; heretofore; before now. qabl do pahar ke . Before noon ( do pahar pahle ).

(Heb. and Chald. ) 1. s. m. The anteriour part, the front. 2. adj. First. 3. adv. Before. قبل ازين ḳabl az-īn, Before this, heretofore. ¶ ḳibal, 1. Power. 2. Plenty. 3. Aside, apart. 4. Presence. ¶ ḳubl or ḳubul, Pudendum viri vel feminæ.
subs: Before that; ante quam; prior to; previously, beforehand
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قبل (qabl), from Arabic قَبْل (qabl).

def: s.f. Grave, tomb
more: n. f. A grave ( turbat ). 1. Koī kisī kī qabr meṅ nahīṅ jāne kā . Prov. In the grave there is no companionship. 2. Muë kī qabr, aur jīte kā ghar . Prov. For dead men a grave, the living houses crave. 3. Ilāhī! terī qabr meṅ kīṛe paṛeṅ! Mah. Wom God grant that worms may prey on you in your grave! 4. Ek dafa to qabar meṅ se bhī uṭhā lūṅgā . Once at least I will drag you out even from your grave. (Violent enmity). 5. Qabar par qabar nahīṅ hotī . Prov. No one ever raises a tomb over a tomb. (Two in a house can never agree). 6. Qabar kā muṅh jhāṅk āë haiṅ . Prov. I have just peeped into my grave. (I was all but dead). qabr par phūl chaṛhānā , v. a. To strew flowers on one's grave.
(Heb. ) s. f. A grave, a tomb. قبرکن ḳabr-kan, A grave-digger. قبر گاه ḳabr-gāh, s. f. A burying ground. قبرستان ḳabr-istān, s. f. A burying ground.
subs: s.f. Consideration or dues paid to a zamīndār or proprietor (by Muhammadans) for permission to dig a grave on ground belonging to him
'To dig up the dead from the grave'; to rake up old grie vances; to renew old quarrels
To have one foot in the grave, to be on the point of death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْر (qabr), from Arabic قَبْر (qabr).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Variegated, spotted, speckled, mottled; grey, dirty white.
more: adj. S. कर्ब्बुर Grey. Kabrā bakrā, chitle kān, jūṅ kāṅbal par dhaul chitān . Rus. Grey goat with spotted ears so fair, as white checks on black blanket are.

adj. S. कुब्ज, Pālī, khujju . Humpbacked. Dāṅt ṭūt, kāyā kubṛāī, būḍhe bhaë, samajh nā āī . Rus. No teeth in his head, his body humpbacked, An old man grown, yet no sense he has packed. kubṛā-pan , Humpbackedness.

def: n. m. A grave yard; cemetry; burial ground. qabr-salāmī . A fee to the proprietor for permission to dig a grave on ground belonging to him. qabr khodnā, banānā, yā khod denā , v. a. 1. To dig (a grave). 2. ( Slang ) To lay in the grave. 3. To beat one within an inch of his life. qabr ke murde ukhāṛnā, gaṛe koële ukhāṛnā , v. a. lit . dig up the buried dead. To renew old quarrels. qabr meṅ pāon laṭkā'nā , Mah.; jamnā kināre baiṭhnā , Hin., v. n. To have one foot in the grave; to be on the point of death. Qabr meṅ pāoṅ laṭkāë baiṭhe haiṅ . I hang on with one foot in my grave.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْرِسْتَان (qabristān). By surface analysis, قَبْر (qabr) + ـسْتَان (-stān).

def: s.m. Lighting, or getting a light or fire (from another); asking for fire; striking fire.
adv. (dakh.) Till when? how long?
adv. (dakh.) Ever: see کبـﮩو kab-hū.

ety: said to be der. fr. Turk. qāpū, for qapū, 'a gate'; q.d. by which one may arrive at the object of one's desire
def: s.m. Opportunity; possession, hold, grasp, grip; control, authority; command, power, will
more: n. m. 1. Grip; hold; reach ( panjah , 4). 2. Command (اختيار 4, 6). 1. Harchand chāhtā hūṅ ki bolūṅ na yār se, Qābū meṅ apne dil ko na pāūṅ to kyā karūn? Amānat. Howe'er unto my love I wish not aught to say, My heart defies control, I cannot have my way. 2. Jab tak karūṅ 'bābū, bābū,' tab tak karūṅ apne qābū . Prov. While I on you for help call, 'Sir, Sir, Sir,' already see it's brought under my power. 3. Favorable opportunity. qābū pānā , v. n. To get an opportunity; have the whip hand; get the upper hand. qābū chalnā , v. n. To be able to do ( bas chalnā ). qābū se bāhar h. , v. n. To lie beyond one's power or control. Phr. Not to mind the curb; to slip the collar; break loose; get clear off; cast off trammels; run wild; run riot. qābū meṅ , adv. See bas meṅ , 1. qābū meṅ rakhnā , v. a. See bas meṅ rakhnā . Phr. To have under one's thumb; to keep under. qābū meṅ k., yā lānā , 1. See ta…

(from Turk. قاپو ḳāpū or قپو ḳapū, A gate; q. d. by which one may enter or arrive at the object of oneʼs desire) s. m. Power, command, authority, opportunity, possession, will. قابوپانا ḳābū pānā, To get advantage or power (over). قابوپرست ḳābū-parast, adj. Tyrannical. قابوپرستي ḳābū-parast-ī, s. f. Tyranny. قابوچلانا ḳābū chalānā, To exercise oneʼs power, to take advantage. قابوچي ḳābū-chī, adj. Despotick, tyrannical.
subs: (-par), To find or get an opportunity; to get into (one's) power or control, have the whip-hand (over), to be revenged (on)
(apnā), To exercise (one's) power or control (over, -par); to take advantage (of)
v.n. (One's) power or authority, &c. to go or reach; to be able to do or accomplish (syn. bas ćalnā)
v.t. To put (one) into possession (of, -kā), to effectuate possession
v.t. To keep under control or subjection, to control, restrain, &c.
or qābū-meṅ lānā, v.t. To bring under control or subjection, to subdue; to acquire an ascendency over; to bring into (one's) possession or grasp, to obtain or take possession of; to secure
s.m. (local) An astute fellow, a long-headed rascal, a crafty fellow, &c. (see qābūćī)

def: s.m. A gate-keeper, door-keeper; — adj. & s.m. Despotic, tyrannical; — selfish, interested (syn. ḵẖẉud-g̠araẓ); — wily, artful, cunning, crafty; — a selfish person, &c.; a scamp, a good-for-nothing fellow, &c.
more: Mah. Wom. n. m. Cor. of qāpuchī a door-keeper. 1. A doorkeeper ( darbān ). 2. A good-for-nothing. Is mue qābūchī ko kaun pūchhtā hai? Wom.

def: s.m. Azure, blue; — a sort of sheepskin of an azure colour; (local) a crane; — a kind of willow.
more: s. m. Azure, blue; a sort of sheepskin of an azure colour.

def: adj. Blue, azure, cerulean.
s.f. A box or nest for pigeons, a pigeon-house (usually made of reeds, or cane, or bamboo), a dove-cot.
more: s. f. A pigeon-house, &c. See کابوک kābūk.

def: s.f. = kābuk, q.v. s.f. A box or nest for pigeons, a pigeon-house (usually made of reeds, or cane, or bamboo), a dove-cot.
more: s. f. A box or nest for pigeons, a pigeon-house.

def: prop. n. Kābul (or Cabul), the capital of Afgānistan (formerly a province of the Mogal empire).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَابُل (kābul), see there for more.

def: v. a. To comply with.

ety: inf. n. of قبل 'to receive, accept,' &c.
def: s.m. Accepting; acceptance; consent, assent, compliance; concurrence; approbation; recognition, sanction; favourable reception; concession; admission, acknowledgment, confession, avowal; — adj. Accepted; approved; consented, &c.
more: n. m. See انگيکار 1. Acknowledgment; avowal. 2. Accord; concurrence. Gājar kī peṅdī, gulāb kā phūl, Kyoṅ miyāṅ guḍḍe! tumheṅ guṛyā qabūl? Girls' song. A carrot root, and a rose bright red, Sir doll! Miss Dolly will you wed? 3. Favorable reception. 4. Recognition; sanction; confirmation; ratification; approbation. qubūl aur manzūr k. , v. a. To approve and confirm. qubūl jama . The stipulated rent or revenue. qubūl-i-javāb . An answer filed in a suit acknowledging the justice of the demand. qubūl-/?/khat . A written assent or agreement. qubūl-sūrat ; Rus. kabūl-sūrat , adj. Handsome; beautiful ( khūbsūrat ). qubūl k , v. a. See انگيکارک and iqrār k. , 3, 4. 1. To assent; nod assent; say yes. 2. To acquiesce, concur, or coincide with; concede. 3. To receive or entertain (a petition). 4. To recognize; sanction; grant; allow. qubūl na k. , v. a. See inkār k. qubūl h. , v. n. To be accepted, …

1. s. m. Consent, favourable reception, concession, approbation, assent. 2. adj. Accepted, approved, consented, agreed. قبول صورت ḳabūl-ṣūrat, Handsome. قبول صورتي ḳabūl-ṣūrat-ī, s. f. Handsomeness. قبول کرنا ḳabūl karnā, To accept, receive, take, assent, allow, confess, consent, deign, agree to, own, promise. قبول هونا ḳabūl honā, To be accepted, agreed to, &c.
subs: s.m. An answer (filed in a suit) acknowledging the justice of a demand
adj. 'Of approved form,' comely, handsome, beautiful

v.t. To accept, receive, take; to entertain (a petition, &c.); to assent to; consent to; to acquiesce in; to comply with, concede, grant; to recognize, sanction; to admit, acknowledge, own, confess; to approve; to make choice of; to prefer (to, -par)
v.n. To be accepted; to be agreed to, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic قُبُول (qubūl).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Kābul; — a native, or an inhabitant, of Kābul; — s.f. A kind of pea (that came from Kābul)
more: s. f. A kind of pea (that came from Kābul). کابلي مٿي kābul-ī miṭṭī, s. f. Armenian bole.

r: qabūlī, qabūl, lit., chick-pea, khićaṛī, mūng
def: (fr. qabūl), s.f. lit. 'Acceptable, grateful, palatable'; a kind of dish made of rice and gram-pulse (chick-pea) boiled together (it differs from khićaṛī only in chick-pea being used in place of mūng).
more: A. P. n. f. A mess of rice and gram pulse boiled together.
s. f. A kind of food, rice and pulse boiled together.

ety: for A. قبولية qabūlīyat; qabul+abst. aff. īyat
def: s.f. Acceptance or hearing (of prayer by God); an acceptance; a receipt; assent, consent; approbation, liking: a written agreement (to pay rent, &c.), the counterpart of a lease.
more: n. f. 1. An acceptance. 2. The counterpart of a lease; an agreement (to pay rent).
s. f. A written agreement, acceptance, assent, consent.

def: v.t. = qabūl karnā, q.v.s.v. qabūl. s.m. Accepting; acceptance; consent, assent, compliance; concurrence; approbation; recognition, sanction; favourable reception; concession; admission, acknowledgment, confession, avowal; — adj. Accepted; approved; consented, &c.
more: v. a. 1. To accept; receive; approve. Use koī nahīṅ qubūltā. 2. To make a confession ( iqrār k . 3).
v. a. To agree, consent, assent, accept, approve, make choice of.
subs: s.m. An answer (filed in a suit) acknowledging the justice of a demand
adj. 'Of approved form,' comely, handsome, beautiful

v.t. To accept, receive, take; to entertain (a petition, &c.); to assent to; consent to; to acquiesce in; to comply with, concede, grant; to recognize, sanction; to admit, acknowledge, own, confess; to approve; to make choice of; to prefer (to, -par)
v.n. To be accepted; to be agreed to, &c.

def: s.m. The nightmare; — modus certus coeundi.
more: s. m. The nightmare. 2. Modus certus coeundi.

def: s.m. One who rears pigeons; one who keeps sporting pigeons, a pigeon-fancier; (met.) a gallant

One who rears pigeons and bets upon their flight; a pigeon-fancier; pigeon-flyer. kabūtar-bā/?/ī , n. f. Rearing of pigeons; pigeonflying. kabūtar pakaṛnā, yā marnā , v. a. To catch pigeons. kabūtar-khānā , n. m. A dovecote; dove house. Kabūtar-khāne kā sā hāl hai, ek āyā ek chalā . The world's a dovecote; one enters, another goes out. kabūtar kā phūl , n. The snowy, but inodorous blossom of the Nagamallie. kabūtar kā jhāṛ. Justicia nasuta. kabūtar kī jaṛ . The Nagamallic root.(?) kabūtar laṛānā , v. a. To fight pigeons. janglī kabūtar , A wild pigeon.

s. m. A pigeon, dove. کبوتر باز kabūtar-bāz, One who rears pigeons. کبوتر بچه kabūtar-bacha, s. m. 1. A young pigeon. 2. An unripe poppy-head, wrapped up in peasemeal and fried in butter or oil. کبوتر خانه kabūtar-ḵẖāna, s. m. Dovecot, dovehouse.
subs: s.m. A young pigeon; — an unripe poppy-head, wrapped up in peasemeal and fried in butter or oil
The snow-white (but inodorous) blossom of the Naga-mallie, Justicia nasuta
The Naga-mallie root, or Rhinacanthus communis.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَبُوتَر (kabūtar, “pigeon; dove”).

def: s.f. A female pigeon; — (met.) a village, or rustic, dancing-girl.
more: n. f. 1. A she-pigeon. 2. A village dancing woman. ☞ So called in ridicule of her style of dancing in the course of which she draws up the ends of her skirt like a fantail pigeon. Her style is, however, natural and graceful, which is more than can be said of the generally ugly contortions of the artificially taught Nautch women of towns.

ety: inf. n. of قبض 'to take, seize,' &c.
def: s.f. Seizing, catching; seizure, capture; taking possession of; possession; confiscation, sequestration; — contraction; astringency; constipation, costiveness; — a receipt, voucher; — tax, tribute

In the way of seizure or distraint.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَبْض (qabz), from Arabic قَبْض (qabḍ). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi قبض (qbẓ /⁠qabz⁠/).

To reinstate. qabzah pānā , v. n. To enter on possession ( dakhl pānā , 2). qabzah paṭṭī-dārī . Land held in severalty. qabzah-i-judāgānah, yā alahdah . Separate possession; land held in severalty; an independent holding. qabzah-i-chand-rozah . Temporary tenure or possession. qabzah-i-hīn hayātī . A life tenure. qabza-dārī , n. f. Occupancy. qabzah dilānā , v. a. To put in possession. qabzah denā , v. a. To make over charge; to give delivery. qabzah rakhnā , v. a. 1. more: n. f. S. कुच Pālī, kucho . The breasts of a girl on the verge of puberty. 1. Woh kanchan sī uskī kucheṅ lāl lāl, Bharī raṅg se qumqume kī misāl . Mir Hasan. So fair her breasts, with so ruddy a tinge, As the liquid with which you fill a syringe. 2. Raṅg bhabūkā, peṭ mulāyam, aur kuchoṅ meṅ sakhtī Sīne se le nāf talak ek sandal kī sī takhtī hai . Jurat. Her face was fair, her body soft, firm set her bubbies stood, From chest to navel smooth she was as plank of sandal wood.
